summary: Large AI-assisted decomp matching PR touching 92 files across fighter, item, stage, menu/game-mode, library, VI, and baselib areas. The PR body says the author used an AI harness, filtered out matches with regressions, avoided obvious cheats such as inline assembly, and claimed about +0.2% global match-rate improvement. Bot report evidence in the loaded text corpus reports +0.23% matched code, 103 new matches, 7 unmatched-item improvements, and 7 unmatched-item regressions. Review feedback focused on wrong section placement for VI sbss data, raw Fighter pointer arithmetic that should use fields or at least M2C_FIELD, avoiding NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub bodies as matches, missing prototypes, and C99 declaration-rule issues.

key_files.role: Effect cleanup/matching; diff shows Effect_RemoveUserData rewritten with typed Effect pointers, direct HSD_ObjFree(&Effect_AllocData), OSReport, and HSD_ASSERT.
Fighter common matching and review focus; diff implements ftCo_800D9C98 and fn_800DA190, adds include, and review discussed raw Fighter offset writes versus callback fields.
Header prototype update for recovered fn_800DA190 signature.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: UNK_RET fn_800DA190 changed to void fn_800DA190(Fighter_GObj* gobj)

key_files.role: Additional changed files from full metadata: src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c, ftcoll.c, ftdevice.c, ftmaterial.c, gm_1601.c, gm_17C0.c/.h, gm_1832.c/.h, gm_18A5.c/.static.h, gm_1BA8.c/.h, gr/granime.c, grbigblue.c, grcastle.c, gricemt.c, grpura.c/.h, grpushon.h, grrcruise.c, if/ifprize.c, if/soundtest.c, it headers and item files, lb/forward.h, lbaudio_ax.c, lbmthp.c, lbrefract.c, mn/mngallery.c/.h, mn/mnname.c, vi headers, vi0502.c, vi1101.h, sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c.

what_changed: Changed 92 files with 1376 additions and 337 deletions according to metadata.
The PR primarily replaced decomp placeholders or NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies with C implementations across many small functions; bot report lists 103 new matches and 7 improvements in unmatched items.
Several headers were updated from unknown signatures toward typed prototypes, e.g. ftCo_Attack100.h changes fn_800DA190 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_800DA190(Fighter_GObj* gobj).
Some changes cleaned generated-looking code into typed field accesses, e.g. ftCo_DownBound.c replaced M2C_FIELD(fp, s8*, 0x2344) with fp->mv.co.downspot.x4.

Some changes introduced or retained generated-looking tactics, including PAD_STACK in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, goto-heavy control flow, raw pointer/offset access in review hunks, and an empty or NOT_IMPLEMENTED-style function body in at least one reviewed stage function.

The loaded bot report contradicts the author body's statement that regressions were filtered out: it reports 7 regressions in unmatched items, including .sbss/.sdata section regressions and one function regression for fn_800D9CE8.
smart_moves: The author explicitly disclosed AI use, the external harness, limited decomp experience, and uncertainty about quality in the PR body.
The PR used automated match reporting: loaded bot output provides concrete matched-code delta, new-match count, and regression/improvement tables.

Some edits moved toward real project idioms: Effect_RemoveUserData uses HSD_ASSERT instead of a raw __assert expansion, and ftCo_DownBound.c uses a named Fighter move-state field rather than M2C_FIELD.

The author accepted review feedback and proposed harness safeguards: force m2c as a first step and reject raw offset pointer arithmetic, NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs, and C99 loop-variable declarations before accepting writes.

decomp_lessons.lesson: AI-assisted batch matching needs a lint/review gate for decomp-specific slop before PR submission: raw pointer offsets, NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs, C99 declarations, and missing prototypes were called out in this PR's comments.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: author describes AI harness and possible low quality
human_pr_text.md: PsiLupan issue comment about missing prototypes and C99 rule violations
review_comments.md: dwilliams27 response proposing verifier rejection rules

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a known struct field exists, prefer it over raw Fighter offset writes; reviewers specifically identified callback fields such as death2_cb, take_dmg_2_cb, and take_dmg_cb as the intended readable fields for the reviewed ftCo_800D9C98 hunk.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: ribbanya says pointer arithmetic is slop and proper Fighter fields should be used
raw_diff: final excerpt shows fp->death2_cb, fp->take_dmg_2_cb, fp->take_dmg_cb in ftCo_800D9C98

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not convert a decomp placeholder into a function that only contains NOT_IMPLEMENTED and treat it as reviewable matching progress; reviewer suggested restoring the /// # placeholder for grPushOn_802183E4.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: hunk shows added void grPushOn_802183E4(int arg) { NOT_IMPLEMENTED; }

decomp_lessons.lesson: Section placement edits are risky in broad matching PRs; the reviewed VI changes wrongly marked ViCharaDesc statics as SDATA when the reviewer said they belong in sbss.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: vi0401.c This is just wrong. It's sbss, not sdata.
review_comments.md: vi0501.c Same as the other. This is sbss, not sdata.
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists .sbss regressions for vi0401 and vi0501

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated-looking matching tactics such as PAD_STACK, gotos, temporary self-assignments, and local coercions need narrow evidence; this PR contains examples, but the loaded slice does not include reviewer approval for those specific tactics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNPk.c PAD_STACK and goto-heavy functions
raw_diff: ft_0892.c temp local coercion around x2070

naming_conventions.convention: Use local role names like fp for Fighter*, gobj for Fighter_GObj*/HSD_GObj*, da for dat_attrs/ext_attr, and ep for Effect* when the role is clear.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple hunks use Fighter* fp, ftKb_DatAttrs* da, Effect* ep, gobj parameter

naming_conventions.convention: Keep address-style function names such as fn_800DA190 or fn_80105A34 where semantics are not recovered; do not invent semantic names without support.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_800DA190, fn_80105A34, fn_801577B4 implementations retain address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: When a function body proves a signature, update the owning header prototype rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Attack100.h prototype update for fn_800DA190

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Batch candidate generation from AI harness, followed by automated match reporting and regression tables.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: author describes AI automation and overnight runs
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report with matched code/data and regressions

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace raw assert expansion with project macro when file/line/message match: Effect_RemoveUserData changed __assert("eflib.c", 0x35U, "0") to HSD_ASSERT(53, 0).
Typed-field cleanup can preserve or improve match readability: ftCo_DownBound.c used fp->mv.co.downspot.x4 instead of M2C_FIELD at 0x2344.

Some functions used PAD_STACK, gotos, and exact local lifetimes to fit codegen; evidence of their necessity is not included in the slice, so they should be treated as tactics requiring verifier proof rather than style.
Reviewer and author discussion suggests an AI decomp pipeline should run m2c first, then feed its output to LLM cleanup, rather than allowing raw offset slop directly from the LLM.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: ribbanya asks whether functions are run through m2c before LLM
review_comments.md: dwilliams27 says agents have m2c but it was not forced as first step

notes: Changed-file metadata is complete in the loaded slice, but raw diff and text corpus are truncated, so not every file-level change or final-resolution state can be inspected.
The PR was merged, but individual review comments may have been resolved in later commits not visible in the excerpt; resolution is recorded as unknown where final evidence is missing.
Bot report provides concrete match and regression numbers, but the intake did not run tools or independently verify builds/objdiff.
Several lessons are grounded in explicit reviewer comments and loaded standards; broader conclusions about all AI-generated output are avoided.

The loaded final diff excerpt for ftCo_800D9C98 shows callback fields, while the review hunk showed raw offsets; this suggests a cleanup occurred but the slice does not prove full review resolution across all changed files.

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI-assisted batch matching needs decomp-specific lint gates
accepted_candidate_records.text: For AI-assisted decomp batches, add pre-review checks for raw offset pointer arithmetic, NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub bodies, C99 loop-variable declarations, and missing prototypes. PR #2217 review explicitly surfaced these issues, and the author proposed adding verifier rejection logic for them.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer real Fighter fields over raw callback offsets
accepted_candidate_records.text: When decompiling Fighter code, raw writes through ((u8*)fp)+offset should be replaced by known fields when available. In PR #2217, reviewer identified raw offset writes in ftCo_800D9C98 as callback fields such as death2_cb and take_dmg_cb.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800D9C98 uses fp->death2_cb, fp->take_dmg_2_cb, fp->take_dmg_cb

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not submit NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies as matched functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: If a function remains unimplemented, keep the /// # placeholder rather than replacing it with a function containing NOT_IMPLEMENTED. PR #2217 review suggested reverting grPushOn_802183E4 to its placeholder.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: grpushon.c suggestion and NOT_IMPLEMENTED hunk

accepted_candidate_records.title: Broad matching PRs should not chase section placement without ownership proof
accepted_candidate_records.text: Section annotations can create regressions in matching PRs. In PR #2217, VI statics were marked SDATA but reviewer said they belonged in sbss, and the bot report listed related .sbss regressions.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: vi0401.c and vi0501.c sbss-not-sdata comments
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report regressions for vi0401/vi0501 .sbss

source_update_candidates.title: AI-generated decomp output should be gated before review
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an explicit standard or checklist note for AI-assisted matching: generated candidates should be rejected before PR submission if they contain raw offset pointer arithmetic where fields/M2C_FIELD are possible, NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs, C99 loop-variable declarations, or missing prototypes. PR #2217 review and author response identify these as harness-level rejection criteria.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards cover typed fields, truthful headers, and matching tactics, but curator may want an AI-output-specific checklist because this PR directly discusses harness safeguards.

source_update_candidates.title: VI files in PR #2217 had sbss versus sdata section feedback
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2217, reviewer PsiLupan stated that ViCharaDesc statics in src/melee/vi/vi0401.c and src/melee/vi/vi0501.c are sbss, not sdata, for symbols un_804D6F60 and un_804D6F84 respectively.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is path-specific section-placement knowledge grounded in explicit review feedback; curator should verify against current symbols/source before adding as a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: Placeholder versus NOT_IMPLEMENTED body guidance
source_update_candidates.text: Consider making explicit that adding a function body containing only NOT_IMPLEMENTED is not acceptable matching progress; keep the /// # placeholder until an implementation is recovered.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards mention rejecting NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs in this PR's review context, but a concise explicit source standard may help future AI/lint workflows.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the author's claimed no-regression filtering as fact; loaded bot report shows 7 regressions in unmatched items.
Do not infer that all 103 bot-reported matches were accepted cleanly or individually reviewed; merge status is known, but per-symbol validation is not in the loaded evidence.
Do not generalize that AI usage is always accepted; reviewer said AI usage is not a problem as long as it matches and does not do anything too weird, which is conditional.
Do not mark PAD_STACK/goto-heavy tactics from ftKb_SpecialNPk.c as accepted style; no reviewer approval or objdiff necessity evidence is loaded.
Do not assert final resolution of the VI section comments or grpushon placeholder comment because final full diff is not loaded.
